name: Margalida and Victoria Mines Loop
name_original: Balsa de Arres - Mina Victoria - Vilamòs — Ruta circular por el Valle de Aran
description: This difficult 19.8-mile mountain biking route in the Valle de Aran features panoramic views, historic mines, and diverse forests.
tips: It is located at 1,560 meters of altitude. It has grills, a fountain and some tables where you can have a picnic enjoying the views of the small pond.

The engine room was built between 1949 and 1951, inside a compressor was kept that made the pneumatic hammers work (new tools to drill rocks, after the slow hammers and picks used by miners). It received electricity from Bossòst, with a long cable of more than 2300 meters.

It is located in the highest part of the town, so we can see the Maladetas massif from this point. As a curiosity, we can say that together with the Bossòst church, they are the only ones that preserve the only two Romanesque bell towers in the Arán Valley. We can also take a walk around this beautiful little town, surrounded by nature.
Beautiful views of the church of Sant Joan Baptista, which was built halfway between the two towns of Arres de Jos and Arres de Sus. In the previous part, it has a small cemetery.
Really nice small town that has plenty of resupply options and some hotels to sleep in.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The full circular route, covering approximately 31.8 km with significant elevation changes, generally takes around 4 hours and 12 minutes to complete on a mountain bike. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how much time you spend exploring the various points of interest.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Balsa de Arres - Mina Victoria - Vilamòs route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter wide paths ascending through meadows and pastures, as well as shaded fir and pine forests. Be prepared for stony sections, and some descents can be very tortuous with many stones and large potholes, potentially requiring you to dismount. There might also be sections with loose earth and steep inclines.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in both natural beauty and historical sites. Key highlights include the picturesque Arres Pond (Bassa d'Arres) with its stunning views, and the fascinating remains of the Machine Room of the Victoria Mine, offering a glimpse into the region's mining past. You'll also pass by charming religious buildings like the Church of Santa Maria de Vilamòs and the Sant Joan Baptista Church.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: While the overall route is rated as difficult for mountain biking due to its length, elevation gain, and technical sections, parts of it are more accessible. The sections around Arres Pond and the Victoria Mine are described as simpler, with gentle slopes and wide paths, making them potentially suitable for families or less experienced riders looking for a shorter, less demanding experience. However, completing the full circular route requires good fitness and mountain biking skills.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Balsa de Arres - Mina Victoria - Vilamòs trail?
faqs.answer: The route is circular, and a common starting point with parking is near the Arres Pond (Bassa d'Arres). This area also offers a picnic spot, making it a convenient base for your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for mountain biking this route. In spring, the forests are particularly aromatic and lush. Autumn offers pleasant temperatures and beautiful fall foliage. Summer can also be good, but be mindful of warmer temperatures, especially on climbs. Winter typically brings snow, making mountain biking challenging or impossible.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Balsa de Arres - Mina Victoria - Vilamòs mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in the Aran Valley, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through pastures with livestock or in areas with wildlife. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the natural environment.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to mountain bike this circular route through the Aran Valley. The trails are publicly accessible.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or panoramic spots along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The route offers spectacular panoramic views. From Arres Pond (Bassa d'Arres), you'll get expansive vistas towards the Luishon Valley and the Aranese mountains. As you gain elevation, broad views of the mountain ranges separating the Aran Valley from France open up, providing stunning photo opportunities.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other known routes in the Aran Valley?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Mina Margalida, Mina Victoria, Camin Reiau - Etapa 6, and Setau Sagèth trails, among others. This allows for potential variations or longer explorations if you wish to combine routes.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, it's essential to pack appropriately. Bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing layers for changing weather conditions. A helmet is mandatory, and protective gear like gloves and knee pads is highly recommended. Don't forget your phone or GPS device, as the route is well-marked but navigation can be helpful.
